page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 16 physical cores, 2 threads per core, and a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the c4-highcpu-32 demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2196.0 and exceptional text processing capabilities. Multi-core performance is strong, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 16926.0 and high throughput in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. The hardware also excels in single-threaded gzip compression and static web serving, achieving 53.01 GB/sec throughput. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's AMX instructions support efficient low-precision inference, delivering 417.13 tokens/second for small LLM text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highcpu-32 is optimized for compute-heavy workloads where memory requirements are secondary. By providing dedicated CPU resources and high compute density without the overhead of excess RAM or local storage, it minimizes resource waste. This profile is highly suitable for high-performance computing, web serving, text processing, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with 16 physical cores and a 2.3 GHz clock speed, it delivers dedicated CPU allocation without local storage or GPU resources. The instance excels in single-core benchmarks, achieving top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests, alongside strong multi-core performance in decompression and static web serving. Supported by Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, it is highly efficient for compute-intensive tasks. This hardware profile is optimized for workloads such as software compilation, text processing, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference, offering high compute density relative to memory capacity.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and advanced matrix extensions for high-throughput CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 32 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing benchmarks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Strong multi-core performance for decompression and static web serving
Best suited for CPU-intensive workloads, software compilation, and light LLM inference
